nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Craft
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English craft ‘skillful, clever, learned’.
English: habitational name from Croft in Leicestershire. See also   Croft  .
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . See also   Croft  .

variants.name: Croft
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places, for example in Lincolnshire, Lancashire, Herefordshire, and North Yorkshire, all named with Old English croft ‘small arable enclosure (normally adjoining a house)’, or a topographic name for someone who lived by a croft, from Middle English croft.
English: possibly in some cases a variant of   Craft   2.
Americanized form of German, Danish, Swedish, or Jewish   Kraft  . Compare   Kroft   and   Craft  .

variants.name: Crafts
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of Craft , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Crafter
meanings: English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English craft ‘skillful, clever’ in the sense ‘craftsman’. Compare Craft .

variants.name: Kraft
meanings: German (also Kräft), Danish, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a strong man, from Old High German kraft, German Kraft ‘strength, power’. The Swedish name probably originated as a soldier's name. In part, the German and Danish names possibly also derive from a late survival of the same word used as a byname, Old High German Chraft(o), Old Norse Kraptr. See also Craft and Croft .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William de Craft de Turville was born in 1189, in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1242, in Willey, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: William de Craft de Turville est né en 1189, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1242, à Willey, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: William de Craft de Turville wurde 1189 in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1242 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Willey, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: William de Craft de Turville è nato il nel 1189 nel Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1242, nel Willey, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 53 anni.
